response:
A night-shift network engineer monitors an organization's infrastructure, which consists of a series of interconnected servers. The reliability of each server $ S_i $ in the network can be modeled by a continuous random variable $ R_i $ following a normal distribution $ N(\mu_i, \sigma_i^2) $. The engineer also collaborates with the cybersecurity team to mitigate potential threats, which can be modeled as a Poisson process with a rate $ \lambda $ of attacks per hour.

1. Given that the network consists of 10 servers, each with a reliability $ R_i $ where $ \mu_i = 0.98 $ and $ \sigma_i = 0.02 $, calculate the probability that at least 9 out of the 10 servers will have a reliability of at least 0.95 at any given time.

2. If the cybersecurity team detects an average of 3 attacks per hour, determine the probability that exactly 5 attacks will be detected during a 2-hour monitoring period.

sentence

The probability that at least 9 out of 10 servers will have a reliability of at least 0.95 at any given time is calculated using the binomial distribution. The probability of a server having a reliability of at least 0.95 is $ P(R_i \geq 0.95) = 1 - \Phi\left(\frac{0.95 - 0.98}{0.02}\right) $, where $ \Phi $ is the cumulative distribution function of the standard normal distribution. This value is then used to compute the probability of at least 9 successes in 10 trials using the binomial probability formula.

The probability that exactly 5 attacks will be detected during a 2-hour monitoring period is calculated using the Poisson distribution. The probability of exactly 5 attacks in 2 hours is $ P(X = 5) = \frac{e^{-\lambda} \lambda^5}{5!} $, where $ \lambda = 3 $ is the average number of attacks per hour. This value is then used to compute the probability of exactly 5 attacks in 2 hours.

sentence
